chunks.text: As a single human being, we are absolutely small.
The Earth lies in a corner of a galaxy
and the universe holds hundreds of billions of galaxies.
When we imagine that scale, human work seems insignificant.
Yet at the same time
is it not astonishing that we can have consciousness and wonder about the universe?
Ancient philosophers looked up at the night sky and thought.
" Why are we here?
" " Is there meaning for us, who are only one aspect of all this?
" We too, under the night sky, can hold the same question.
Modern science teaches us much.
The universe originated 13.
8 billion years ago.
Stars generate energy by fusion.
Life arose from chemical reactions.
But why does being exist, and why is there this universe rather than nothing?
Science has no definitely clear answer to this root question.
Human consciousness is also mysterious.
Why, from the electrical signals of neurons
do the sensation of " red " or the emotion of " joy " emerge?
Neither psychologist nor philosopher has yet given a clear answer.
To face these questions comes with a painful loneliness.
But it is also a privilege given only to human beings.
Though we are small, we can think about the universe itself.
Is there anything more extraordinary than this?
